title: Yeast Crude Protein Extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7igd8","text":"Quick crude protein extraction from budding or fission yeast.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"jmf2","text":"2-mercaptoethanol (also known as β-mercaptoethanol) is toxic and must be handled under a fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grow cells to mid-exponential phase in liquid medium.
Normalize cells to an OD 600 of 2-3 in 1 mL of H 2 O.
Centrifuge 1 mL of H 2 O containing your cells at 9000 RPM for two minutes. 00:02:00
Resuspend pelleted cells in 100 uL 0.2 M NaOH and incubate on ice for 15 minutes. undetermined 00:15:00
Centrifuge cells at 9000 RPM for two minutes. 00:02:00
Resuspend the pellet in 100 uL of Yeast Protein Extraction Sample Buffer. undetermined Yeast Protein Extraction Sample Buffer by Alan Cone
Add 0.4 uL 2-mercaptoethanol to the mixture. undetermined
Boil cells in sample buffer for 5 minutes at 95 C. 00:05:00
Can store at -80 C or load supernatant into a gel.
Vortex briefly then centrifuge at 10,000 RPM for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
creator: Alan Cone
